title: DBlockedWalks
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- The walk `π` is *$C$-$d$-blocked* (LN def 3.19, item 1):
either $v_0$ or $v_n$ lies in `C`, or some interior
non-collider position on `π` lies in `C`, or some collider
position on `π` lies outside $\Anc^G(C)$. -/
def IsDBlocked {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) : Prop :=
  (π.nodeAt 0 ∈ C) ∨
  (π.nodeAt π.length ∈ C) ∨
  (∃ k, 0 < k ∧ k < π.length ∧ π.IsNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) ∨
  (∃ k, π.IsColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∉ G.AncSet C)

/-- The walk `π` is *$C$-$d$-open* (LN def 3.19, item 2):
the literal negation of `π.IsDBlocked C`. -/
def IsDOpen {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) : Prop :=
  ¬ π.IsDBlocked C

/-- Defining equation for `IsDOpen`. The LN-original
characterisation: "$\pi$ is $C$-$d$-open iff it is not
$C$-$d$-blocked". Holds by definition. -/
theorem isDOpen_iff_not_isDBlocked {v w : α}
    (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) :
    π.IsDOpen C ↔ ¬ π.IsDBlocked C :=
  Iff.rfl
lean_code_without_comments:
def IsDBlocked {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) : Prop :=
  (π.nodeAt 0 ∈ C) ∨
  (π.nodeAt π.length ∈ C) ∨
  (∃ k, 0 < k ∧ k < π.length ∧ π.IsNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) ∨
  (∃ k, π.IsColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∉ G.AncSet C)

def IsDOpen {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) : Prop :=
  ¬ π.IsDBlocked C

theorem isDOpen_iff_not_isDBlocked {v w : α}
    (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) :
    π.IsDOpen C ↔ ¬ π.IsDBlocked C :=
  Iff.rfl
lean_explanation:
The walk `π : Walk G v w` runs between endpoints `v, w : α`, where `α : Type*` is the ambient vertex type. The graph `G : CDMG α` is a chain directed mixed graph (the LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$); the conditioning set is `C : Set α`, with no subtype wrapper enforcing the LN's prose-level preamble $C \subseteq J \cup V$. Interior positions are indexed by `k : ℕ` and bounded by `0 < k ∧ k < π.length`, picking out the genuinely interior positions of $\pi = (v_0, v_1, \ldots, v_n)$.

`π.IsDBlocked C` is a `Prop`-valued four-way disjunction matching the LN's reading order. The two endpoint clauses use `π.nodeAt 0 ∈ C` and `π.nodeAt π.length ∈ C` (the LN's $v_0 \in C$ and $v_n \in C$). The interior non-collider clause is an existential over positions $k$ satisfying both `π.IsNonColliderAt k` — which collapses the LN's left-chain, right-chain, and fork sub-patterns into a single non-collider predicate — together with $v_k \in C$. The collider clause is an existential over positions $k$ with `π.IsColliderAt k` and $v_k \notin G.\mathrm{AncSet}\ C$, Lean's name for the LN's $\Anc^G(C)$ supplied by the family-reachability module `Section3_1.FamilyReachability`.

Lean's dot-notation `π.IsDBlocked C` works because `π` is the first explicit argument, the same convention used throughout chapter 3 (`π.IsColliderAt`, `π.IsSigmaOpen`, and so on).

`π.IsDOpen C` is the literal Lean negation `¬ π.IsDBlocked C`; the LN itself defines $d$-open as 'not $d$-blocked', and the companion lemma `isDOpen_iff_not_isDBlocked` is consequently `Iff.rfl`.
design_choices:
**The four-disjunct shape mirrors the LN's reading order rather than the collapsed two-disjunct form.** A reader of the lecture notes sees clause (i) — two endpoint sub-conditions — followed by clause (ii) — four sub-patterns (left chain, right chain, fork, collider). The first three sub-patterns of (ii) all read '$v_k \in C$' under a non-collider shape and together exhaust 'interior non-collider in $C$'; the fourth is the collider sub-clause. The Lean definition keeps this one-to-one correspondence: endpoint $v_0$, endpoint $v_n$, interior non-collider, collider. The natural alternative is a two-disjunct form ('some non-collider position is in $C$, or some collider position is not in $\Anc^G(C)$'), equivalent under the observation that endpoints are always non-colliders. We reject the collapse because the very next row (claim 3.28) is the LN's own statement of that equivalence; baking it into the definition would silently delete an articulated theorem.

**The interior bound `0 < k ∧ k < π.length` appears on the non-collider disjunct but not the collider disjunct.** With our `IsNonColliderAt`, endpoint positions are unconditionally non-colliders, so dropping the bound would let any walk with $v_0 \in C$ trigger the non-collider clause and collapse the four-disjunct shape. The collider predicate, by contrast, is already `False` at $k = 0$, $k = \pi.\mathrm{length}$, and at out-of-range positions, so any witness is automatically interior; a redundant bound would only burden downstream proofs with an extra discharge step.

**`IsNonColliderAt` rather than `IsBlockableNonColliderAt`.** This is the principal shape-level difference between $d$-blocking and $\sigma$-blocking (def 3.17). $\sigma$-blocking restricts attention to *blockable* non-colliders — a sub-classification driven by the coreflexive-edge boundary of the CDMG — and treats non-blockable non-colliders as '$\sigma$-open for free'. $d$-blocking imposes no such restriction: every non-collider position is a potential blocker. Using `IsBlockableNonColliderAt` here would silently identify def 3.19 with def 3.17, which is wrong even though under acyclicity (claim 3.20) the two collapse to the same family of walks.

**`IsDOpen` is the syntactic negation of `IsDBlocked`, not a parallel positive predicate.** The LN itself flips the asymmetry it used for $\sigma$-separation: $d$-open is defined in one line as 'not $d$-blocked'. We mirror that — `IsDOpen := ¬ IsDBlocked` — which makes `isDOpen_iff_not_isDBlocked` definitionally `Iff.rfl` and eliminates the classical-reasoning step the $\sigma$-version's De-Morgan dual requires. A caller wanting the positive conjunctive shape ('every endpoint not in $C$, every interior non-collider not in $C$, every collider in $\Anc^G(C)$') applies De Morgan on demand; we judged that cost worth paying to keep the LN's framing as the primitive shape.

**`C : Set α` rather than a `{ C // C ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V }` subtype.** The LN's preamble '$C \subseteq J \cup V$' is stated in prose, not enforced at the type level. `AncSet G C` already silently ignores members outside the graph, and threading a subtype through every downstream signature (def 3.20, the claim 3.28 / 29 / 30 chain, chapter $\ge 4$ Markov properties, do-calculus) would impose a real ergonomic cost for no proof benefit. Consumers who genuinely depend on the side-condition state it as a separate hypothesis at the call site.
